prompt: I'm building a NIP-05 identity provider service. I need a dynamic Node.js server that handles the /.well-known/nostr.json endpoint for multiple users. It should support the ?name= query parameter, include relay hints in the response, validate the local-part format, and handle the root identifier (_@domain). Give me the complete server code and explain the nostr.json response format.
assertions: The path '/.well-known/nostr.json' appears in a route or handler definition
The query parameter 'name' is read from the request URL
Both 'names' and 'relays' appear in the JSON response structure
The 'relays' values are arrays of WebSocket URLs starting with 'wss://'
The string 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' appears in the response headers
A regex or character validation restricts local-part to lowercase alphanumeric characters
The underscore '_' character is handled as the root identifier for the domain
All example pubkeys in the output are 64-character hex strings not npub
